input_args.parameters_json_dump: '{"abstract": "Many species of migratory birds are declining worldwide, including throughout North America. Some of the most cited causes of decline are linked to climate change, urbanization, and growth in agriculture. Across eastern Canada, a number of insecticides and herbicides are commonly sprayed before and during the grain growing season to control pests and foliage competitors. During wintering and migration, a declining Arctic-breeding songbird, the snow bunting (Plectrophenax nivalis), utilizes open farmlands of southern Canada; therefore, this could be a period when the species is most exposed to these pesticides. We tested snow bunting tissues (blood and liver) for the 4 pesticides most commonly used in grain agriculture in Canada: atrazine, chlothianidin, imidacloprid, and glyphosate, as well as a glyphosate derivative (aminomethylphosphic acid, AMPA). Although this species is thought to forage in grain fields during autumn through spring, we found no detectable traces of any of the five substances. Wintering buntings may either not be exposed to these pesticides during their presence in agriculture fields or, given the rapid turnover of these pesticides in the blood and tissues, be exposed to doses below detection level in samples. Aucun pesticide commun d\\u00c3\\u00a9tect\\u00c3\\u00a9 chez les Plectrophanes des neiges qui fr\\u00c3\\u00a9quentent des milieux agricoles de l\'Est du Qu\\u00c3\\u00a9bec R\\u00c3\\u2030SUM\\u00c3\\u2030. De nombreuses esp\\u00c3\\u00a8ces d\'oiseaux migrateurs sont en baisse dans le monde entier, y compris en Am\\u00c3\\u00a9rique du Nord. Certaines des causes les plus cit\\u00c3\\u00a9es pour expliquer cette baisse sont li\\u00c3\\u00a9es aux changements climatiques, \\u00c3\\u00a0 l\'urbanisation et \\u00c3\\u00a0 l\'expansion de l\'agriculture. Dans l\'Est du Canada, des insecticides et des herbicides sont couramment pulv\\u00c3\\u00a9ris\\u00c3\\u00a9s avant et pendant la saison de croissance des c\\u00c3\\u00a9r\\u00c3\\u00a9ales pour lutter contre les parasites et les plantes comp\\u00c3\\u00a9titrices. Durant l\'hivernage et la migration, le Plectrophane des neiges (Plectrophenax nivalis), oiseau chanteur en diminution qui niche dans l\'Arctique, fr\\u00c3\\u00a9quente les terres agricoles du sud du Canada; par cons\\u00c3\\u00a9quent, ces p\\u00c3\\u00a9riodes pourraient \\u00c3\\u00aatre celles o\\u00c3\\u00b9 l\'esp\\u00c3\\u00a8ce est la plus expos\\u00c3\\u00a9e \\u00c3\\u00a0 ces pesticides. Nous avons test\\u00c3\\u00a9 certains tissus du plectrophane (sang et foie) pour les 4 pesticides les plus couramment utilis\\u00c3\\u00a9s dans l\'agriculture c\\u00c3\\u00a9r\\u00c3\\u00a9ali\\u00c3\\u00a8re au Canada : l\'atrazine, la chlothianidine, l\'imidaclopride et le glyphosate, ainsi qu\'un d\\u00c3\\u00a9riv\\u00c3\\u00a9 du glyphosate (l\'acide aminom\\u00c3\\u00a9thylphosphonique, AMPA). Bien que l\'on pense que cette esp\\u00c3\\u00a8ce se nourrisse dans les champs de c\\u00c3\\u00a9r\\u00c3\\u00a9ales de l\'automne au printemps, nous n\'avons trouv\\u00c3\\u00a9 aucune trace d\\u00c3\\u00a9tectable de l\'une de ces cinq substances. Les plectrophanes hivernant peuvent soit ne pas \\u00c3\\u00aatre expos\\u00c3\\u00a9s \\u00c3\\u00a0 ces pesticides lorsqu\'ils sont pr\\u00c3\\u00a9sents dans les champs agricoles, soit \\u00c3\\u00aatre expos\\u00c3\\u00a9s \\u00c3\\u00a0 des doses inf\\u00c3\\u00a9rieures au niveau de d\\u00c3\\u00a9tection dans les \\u00c3\\u00a9chantillons \\u00c3\\u00a9tant donn\\u00c3\\u00a9 la d\\u00c3\\u00a9gradation rapide de ces pesticides dans le sang et les tissus.", "max_output_tokens": 4096, "model": "gpt-5-mini", "reasoning": {"effort": "low"}, "system_message": "You are EcodataGPT, a structured data extraction engine.\\n\\nGoal: extract fields from the user\'s abstract into the provided schema.\\n\\nRules:\\n- Only use information explicitly supported by the text. Do NOT guess or infer.\\n- If a field is not clearly stated, set it to null (or an empty list) as allowed by the schema.\\n- Prefer conservative outputs over over-extraction.\\n- Output must conform exactly to the schema (types, enums, lists).\\n\\n## Modulator Boolean Fields\\n\\nSet each to true only when the text provides explicit evidence; otherwise set to null.\\n\\n- **time_series**: true if the dataset contains repeated measurements at the same locations/populations over time (e.g. \\"annual surveys from 2005 to 2015\\", \\"monitored monthly\\"). A single snapshot is NOT a time series.\\n- **multispecies**: true if the dataset covers more than one species or taxonomic group.\\n- **threatened_species**: true if any studied species are described as threatened, endangered, vulnerable, at-risk, or listed under IUCN/CITES/national red lists.\\n- **new_species_science**: true if the study describes or names a species new to science (newly described taxon).\\n- **new_species_region**: true if the study reports a species recorded in a region for the first time (range extension, first regional record).\\n- **bias_north_south**: true if the text explicitly discusses geographic bias toward the Global North or underrepresentation of the Global South.", "temperature": null, "text_format": "{\\"$defs\\": {\\"DataSource\\":
